tips.title: Right-size underutilized Virtual Machines
tips.description: Azure Advisor identifies VMs with average CPU below 5% or network utilization below 7 Mbps over 7 days. Switch to a smaller VM size or deallocate idle VMs to reduce compute costs.

tips.title: Purchase Reserved Instances for steady workloads
tips.description: Azure Reserved VM Instances provide up to 72% discount over pay-as-you-go pricing with 1-year or 3-year commitments. Best for production workloads with predictable usage patterns.

tips.title: Use Azure Savings Plans for flexible compute
tips.description: Azure Savings Plans for Compute offer up to 65% savings with hourly spend commitment that applies across VM series, regions, and operating systems. More flexible than Reserved Instances.

tips.title: Use Spot VMs for interruptible workloads
tips.description: Azure Spot VMs offer up to 90% discount for workloads that can tolerate interruption — batch processing, dev/test, CI/CD pipelines, and stateless microservices.

tips.title: Apply Azure Hybrid Benefit for Windows and SQL
tips.description: Bring existing Windows Server and SQL Server licenses with Software Assurance to Azure. Saves up to 85% on Windows VMs and up to 55% on Azure SQL compared to pay-as-you-go.

tips.title: Auto-shutdown non-production VMs
tips.description: Configure auto-shutdown schedules for dev/test/staging VMs during off-hours and weekends. Use Azure Automation or VM auto-shutdown feature to stop VMs when not needed.

tips.title: Switch to burstable B-series VMs for variable workloads
tips.description: B-series VMs provide a baseline CPU with burst capability. Ideal for web servers, small databases, and dev environments that don't need continuous full CPU — costs 40-60% less than general-purpose VMs.

tips.title: Use Ephemeral OS Disks for stateless VMs
tips.description: Ephemeral OS disks use the VM's local storage at no additional cost, eliminating managed disk charges for the OS disk. Best for stateless nodes, VMSS, and container hosts.

tips.title: Configure autoscaling rules for VM Scale Sets
tips.description: Set scale-in and scale-out rules based on CPU, memory, or custom metrics. Use scheduled scaling for predictable load patterns and configure aggressive scale-in to avoid running excess instances.

tips.title: Mix Spot and on-demand instances in VMSS
tips.description: Use Spot priority VMs in VM Scale Sets with on-demand fallback for high availability. Configure eviction policies and max price to optimize cost while maintaining workload resilience.

tips.title: Scale VMSS to zero during off-peak hours
tips.description: For non-production workloads, configure scheduled autoscale to reduce VMSS capacity to zero instances overnight and on weekends. Re-scale before business hours begin.

tips.title: Use Consumption Plan for sporadic workloads
tips.description: Azure Functions Consumption Plan charges only for execution time and memory used. Avoid Premium or Dedicated plans for workloads with fewer than 1M executions/month or sporadic invocation patterns.

tips.title: Optimize function memory allocation and execution time
tips.description: Profile function memory usage and reduce allocation to match actual needs. Optimize code to reduce execution duration — billing is per GB-second. Use async patterns to avoid idle waits.

tips.title: Use Premium Plan with reserved instances for steady Functions
tips.description: For Functions that run continuously or need VNET integration, use Premium Plan with pre-warmed instances and apply Reserved Instance pricing for predictable monthly savings.

tips.title: Scale down idle App Service plans
tips.description: Downgrade underutilized App Service plans from Premium to Standard or Basic tier. Consider consolidating multiple low-traffic apps into a single plan.

tips.title: Consolidate apps into shared App Service Plans
tips.description: Multiple App Service apps can share a single plan's compute resources. Consolidate low-traffic apps to avoid paying for separate compute instances per application.

tips.title: Use Reserved Instances for production App Service Plans
tips.description: Reserve Premium v3 and Isolated v2 App Service Plan instances for 1 or 3 years. Provides up to 55% discount over pay-as-you-go for stable production web applications.

tips.title: Use autoscaling rules for App Service Plans
tips.description: Configure scale-out and scale-in rules based on HTTP queue length, CPU, or schedule. Avoid running maximum instances 24/7 when peak traffic only occurs during business hours.

tips.title: Use Container Instances for short-lived batch jobs
tips.description: ACI bills per-second for CPU and memory. Use it for short-lived tasks instead of maintaining always-on VMs. Ideal for build agents, data processing, and scheduled jobs under 10 minutes.

tips.title: Minimize ACI resource allocations
tips.description: Only allocate the CPU and memory that your container actually needs. Each additional vCPU or GB of memory adds to the per-second cost. Profile containers to find the minimum viable resources.

tips.title: Use Spot containers for interruptible ACI workloads
tips.description: ACI Spot containers offer significant discounts for fault-tolerant workloads. Configure restart policies and use container groups with multiple containers sharing resources to optimize costs.

tips.title: Right-size AKS node pools and enable cluster autoscaler
tips.description: Enable AKS cluster autoscaler to scale node pools based on pod demand. Use Virtual Nodes for burst workloads and configure resource requests/limits to improve bin-packing efficiency.

tips.title: Use Spot node pools for interruptible AKS workloads
tips.description: Add Spot VM node pools to AKS clusters for batch processing, dev/test, and stateless workloads. Configure pod anti-affinity and disruption budgets for workload resilience.

tips.title: Scale down AKS dev/test clusters outside business hours
tips.description: Use KEDA or scheduled autoscaler to scale dev/test node pools to minimum (or zero) after hours. Use AKS Start/Stop feature to completely deallocate non-production clusters overnight.

tips.title: Use AKS Free tier for dev/test clusters
tips.description: AKS Free tier provides cluster management at no cost for dev/test. Reserve Paid (Standard/Premium) tier for production where SLA guarantees and advanced features are required.

tips.title: Right-size Azure SQL databases
tips.description: Scale down DTU or vCore tiers for databases with consistently low CPU and IO utilization. Use elastic pools to share resources across multiple databases with variable usage patterns.

tips.title: Use Azure SQL Reserved Capacity
tips.description: Reserve vCore compute capacity for Azure SQL Database or Managed Instance for 1 or 3 years. Provides significant discounts over pay-as-you-go pricing for predictable database workloads.

tips.title: Use Elastic Pools for multi-tenant databases
tips.description: Azure SQL Elastic Pools share eDTU/vCore resources across multiple databases. Ideal when databases have variable usage patterns with different peak times, reducing per-database costs significantly.

tips.title: Use serverless tier for intermittent SQL workloads
tips.description: Azure SQL Serverless auto-pauses the database after a configurable idle period and auto-resumes on connection. Only pay for compute when active — ideal for dev/test and intermittent workloads.

tips.title: Apply Azure Hybrid Benefit to Azure SQL
tips.description: Use existing SQL Server core licenses with active Software Assurance on Azure SQL Database and Managed Instance. Saves up to 55% on vCore-based compute pricing.

tips.title: Use autoscale throughput for variable Cosmos DB workloads
tips.description: Switch from manual provisioned throughput to autoscale to automatically adjust RU/s between 10% and 100% of max. Avoid paying for peak capacity during off-peak periods.

tips.title: Purchase Cosmos DB Reserved Capacity
tips.description: Reserve Cosmos DB throughput (RU/s) for 1 or 3 years to receive up to 65% discount over pay-as-you-go pricing. Apply to production databases with predictable RU/s requirements.

tips.title: Use Cosmos DB serverless for low-traffic applications
tips.description: Cosmos DB serverless mode charges only for RUs consumed per request with no minimum commitment. Ideal for dev/test, prototypes, and applications with fewer than 5000 RU/s average throughput.

tips.title: Configure TTL to auto-expire stale Cosmos DB data
tips.description: Set Time-to-Live (TTL) on containers to automatically delete documents after a specified period. Reduces storage costs and keeps throughput efficient for session data, logs, and temporary records.

tips.title: Right-size PostgreSQL Flexible Server tiers
tips.description: Monitor CPU and memory utilization and downgrade from General Purpose to Burstable tier for databases under 20% average CPU. Reduce vCores and storage to match actual workload requirements.

tips.title: Stop PostgreSQL Flexible Server when not in use
tips.description: Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server supports stop/start. Schedule stops for dev/test databases during off-hours to eliminate compute charges while retaining data.

tips.title: Use Reserved Capacity for PostgreSQL
tips.description: Commit to 1 or 3-year reservations for Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server compute. Provides up to 60% discount over pay-as-you-go for production databases.

tips.title: Downgrade MySQL Flexible Server compute tier
tips.description: Switch from General Purpose to Burstable tier for dev/test MySQL databases. Monitor InnoDB buffer pool hit ratio and CPU to validate smaller tier handles the workload adequately.

tips.title: Stop MySQL Flexible Server during idle periods
tips.description: Use the stop/start capability of MySQL Flexible Server for non-production instances. Compute charges stop immediately while storage and backups remain intact.

tips.title: Purchase Reserved Capacity for MySQL
tips.description: Commit to 1 or 3-year reservations for MySQL Flexible Server compute resources. Provides up to 60% savings over pay-as-you-go pricing for steady-state production databases.

tips.title: Move infrequently accessed blobs to Cool or Archive tier
tips.description: Azure Blob Storage offers Hot, Cool, Cold, and Archive tiers. Move data accessed less than once per month to Cool (50% cheaper) or Archive (90% cheaper) with lifecycle management policies.

tips.title: Configure lifecycle management policies for blob data
tips.description: Automate tier transitions and deletion using lifecycle management rules. Set rules to move blobs to Cool after 30 days, Archive after 90 days, and delete after retention period expires.

tips.title: Delete old blob snapshots and versions
tips.description: Blob versioning and snapshots accumulate storage costs over time. Configure lifecycle rules to delete versions and snapshots older than your retention policy requires.

tips.title: Use Reserved Capacity for predictable storage volumes
tips.description: Azure Storage Reserved Capacity offers up to 38% discount for committing to 1 or 3 years of storage capacity (100TB or 1PB) on Hot or Cool tiers.

tips.title: Delete unattached Managed Disks
tips.description: Orphaned managed disks (not attached to any VM) continue incurring charges. Identify and delete unused disks or snapshot them to a cheaper tier before deletion.

tips.title: Downgrade Premium SSD to Standard SSD for non-critical disks
tips.description: Use Standard SSD (or Standard HDD for archival) for non-production and low-IOPS workloads. Premium SSD is only necessary for latency-sensitive production databases and applications.

tips.title: Right-size disk capacity to actual usage
tips.description: Azure bills for provisioned disk size, not used space. Shrink oversized disks by creating a smaller disk from snapshot. Use monitoring to track actual disk space utilization.

tips.title: Use transaction-optimized or Cool tier for Azure Files
tips.description: Azure Files offers Premium, Transaction-optimized, Hot, and Cool tiers. Move file shares with low access frequency to Cool tier. Reserve Premium only for latency-sensitive workloads.

tips.title: Use Azure File Sync to tier cold data to cloud
tips.description: Azure File Sync keeps frequently accessed files on-premises while tiering cold data to Azure Files. Reduces on-premises storage costs while maintaining fast access to hot data.

tips.title: Apply lifecycle policies to Data Lake Storage Gen2
tips.description: Data Lake Storage Gen2 supports the same Hot/Cool/Archive tiers as Blob Storage. Configure lifecycle management to tier aging analytics data to cheaper storage classes automatically.

tips.title: Compress and optimize file formats in Data Lake
tips.description: Store data in columnar formats (Parquet, Delta) with compression instead of raw CSV/JSON. Reduces storage volume by 60-80% and improves query performance for analytics workloads.

tips.title: Delete temporary and staging data in Data Lake
tips.description: Implement cleanup pipelines to remove temporary ETL staging data, failed job outputs, and intermediate processing files that accumulate in Data Lake Storage directories.

tips.title: Optimize Log Analytics data ingestion
tips.description: Reduce Log Analytics costs by filtering verbose logs at collection, setting appropriate retention periods, using Basic Logs tier for debug data, and archiving old data to cheaper storage.

tips.title: Use commitment tiers for Log Analytics
tips.description: Log Analytics commitment tiers (100GB/day+) provide up to 30% discount over pay-as-you-go per-GB pricing. Analyze daily ingestion volume to select the optimal commitment level.

tips.title: Use Basic Logs for high-volume diagnostic data
tips.description: Basic Logs plan reduces ingestion costs by ~60% for verbose diagnostic logs you rarely query. Trade interactive query capabilities for significantly lower per-GB costs.

tips.title: Reduce workspace data retention to minimum required
tips.description: Default retention is 30 days (free). Additional retention costs per-GB/month. Set retention to compliance minimums and archive older data to Storage Account for long-term needs.

tips.title: Filter out unnecessary log sources and tables
tips.description: Disable collection of high-volume, low-value log tables (e.g., ContainerLog stdout for healthy pods, SecurityEvent noise). Use workspace transformations to filter rows before ingestion.

tips.title: Configure sampling to reduce Application Insights volume
tips.description: Enable adaptive or fixed-rate sampling to reduce telemetry volume by 50-90% while preserving statistical accuracy. Most apps don't need 100% of request/dependency telemetry captured.

tips.title: Set daily cap on Application Insights ingestion
tips.description: Configure a daily ingestion cap to prevent unexpected cost spikes from verbose logging or traffic bursts. Set alerts when approaching the cap to avoid data loss.

tips.title: Move to workspace-based Application Insights
tips.description: Workspace-based Application Insights benefits from Log Analytics commitment tier pricing and data archival options. Migrate from classic resources to gain cost management features.

tips.title: Delete unused Public IP addresses
tips.description: Unassociated Standard Public IP addresses incur hourly charges. Remove unused public IPs identified by Azure Advisor or use dynamic allocation where static IPs aren't required.

tips.title: Remove idle Load Balancers
tips.description: Standard Load Balancers charge per hour and per rule even without backend traffic. Delete unused load balancers and consolidate multiple load balancers where possible.

tips.title: Use VNet peering instead of VPN Gateway for Azure-to-Azure
tips.description: VNet peering provides lower latency and higher bandwidth than VPN Gateway for intra-Azure connectivity. Peering is billed per-GB vs. VPN Gateway hourly charges plus data transfer.

tips.title: Right-size Application Gateway capacity units
tips.description: Use autoscaling Application Gateway v2 instead of fixed capacity. Set appropriate min/max instance counts based on traffic patterns to avoid paying for unused capacity units.

tips.title: Delete unused Application Gateway instances
tips.description: Application Gateway v2 charges per hour plus capacity units even without traffic. Remove gateways for decommissioned applications or consolidate apps behind fewer gateways.

tips.title: Right-size ExpressRoute circuit bandwidth
tips.description: Monitor ExpressRoute circuit utilization and downgrade bandwidth tier if consistently below 30% usage. Consider Local SKU for same-metro peering which eliminates data transfer charges.

tips.title: Use ExpressRoute Local for same-metro connectivity
tips.description: ExpressRoute Local SKU eliminates per-GB egress charges for circuits connected at peering locations in the same Azure metro. Unlimited data at a lower monthly circuit fee.

tips.title: Reduce egress costs with Azure CDN or Front Door
tips.description: Data egress from Azure to the internet is metered. Use Azure CDN or Front Door to cache static content closer to users. CDN egress is cheaper per-GB than direct blob/compute egress.

tips.title: Co-locate resources in the same region to avoid cross-region transfer
tips.description: Inter-region data transfer charges apply for resources in different Azure regions. Place dependent services (app + database + cache) in the same region to eliminate cross-region bandwidth costs.

tips.title: Downgrade VPN Gateway SKU for low-throughput connections
tips.description: VPN Gateway SKUs range from Basic to VpnGw5. Downgrade to a lower SKU if actual throughput is well below the current tier's capacity. Basic SKU suits dev/test scenarios.

tips.title: Delete unused VPN Gateway connections
tips.description: VPN Gateways charge hourly regardless of traffic. Remove gateways for decommissioned site-to-site connections or replaced ExpressRoute circuits to stop incurring idle charges.

tips.title: Use self-hosted agents for high-volume CI/CD pipelines
tips.description: Microsoft-hosted agents charge per-minute for parallel jobs. Use self-hosted agents on existing VMs or Spot instances for pipelines running hundreds of minutes daily to reduce costs.

tips.title: Clean up old build artifacts and test attachments
tips.description: Azure DevOps storage is free up to 2GB then charged per-GB. Set retention policies on build pipelines, delete old artifacts, and purge test attachments to stay within free tier.

tips.title: Audit and remove inactive Azure DevOps users
tips.description: Basic plan users cost per-user/month. Audit user access levels, downgrade inactive users to Stakeholder (free), and remove departed team members to reduce licensing costs.

tips.title: Optimize Azure AD Premium license assignments
tips.description: Assign P1/P2 licenses only to users who need premium features (Conditional Access, PIM, Identity Protection). Use group-based licensing to automate and audit assignments.

tips.title: Remove stale guest accounts and app registrations
tips.description: Inactive guest users and orphaned app registrations can accumulate licensing costs. Audit last sign-in dates and remove accounts inactive for 90+ days using access reviews.

tips.title: Use Standard tier Key Vault unless HSM is required
tips.description: Key Vault Premium tier (HSM-backed keys) costs significantly more. Use Standard tier for secrets and certificates unless regulatory compliance mandates hardware security modules.

tips.title: Delete unused Key Vault secrets and expired certificates
tips.description: While Key Vault charges per-operation not per-secret, unnecessary vaults incur base costs. Consolidate secrets into fewer vaults and purge soft-deleted vaults after retention period.

tips.title: Right-size Redis cache tier and memory
tips.description: Monitor cache hit ratio, memory usage, and connected clients. Downgrade from Premium to Standard tier or reduce cache size if memory utilization is consistently below 50%.

tips.title: Use Reserved Capacity for production Redis instances
tips.description: Commit to 1 or 3-year reservations for Azure Cache for Redis to receive up to 55% discount over pay-as-you-go. Apply to stable production caching layers with predictable capacity needs.

tips.title: Delete unused Redis instances in non-production environments
tips.description: Dev/test Redis instances often remain running after project completion. Identify caches with near-zero operations and delete or consolidate them to reduce monthly costs.

tips.title: Right-size Event Hubs throughput units
tips.description: Monitor incoming and outgoing bytes/events to determine actual throughput needs. Reduce throughput units (TUs) for namespaces running below 50% capacity or enable auto-inflate selectively.

tips.title: Use Basic tier for simple event streaming scenarios
tips.description: Event Hubs Basic tier costs significantly less than Standard. Use Basic for scenarios not requiring consumer groups, capture, or partition keys — suitable for simple telemetry ingestion.

tips.title: Delete unused Event Hub namespaces
tips.description: Event Hubs namespaces incur hourly charges for allocated TUs regardless of message volume. Remove namespaces for decommissioned event streams or consolidate hubs into fewer namespaces.

tips.title: Downgrade Service Bus tier for low-volume messaging
tips.description: Service Bus Premium tier includes dedicated capacity but costs significantly more. Use Standard tier for workloads not requiring message isolation, large messages, or geo-disaster recovery.

tips.title: Remove unused Service Bus queues and topics
tips.description: Unused Premium tier messaging units incur costs regardless of message flow. Delete unused queues, topics, and entire namespaces for decommissioned microservice communication patterns.

tips.title: Set message TTL and dead-letter policies
tips.description: Configure message Time-to-Live and max delivery counts to prevent queue bloat. Route dead-lettered messages to cheaper storage for analysis instead of keeping them in Service Bus indefinitely.

tips.title: Pause dedicated SQL pools when not in use
tips.description: Synapse dedicated SQL pools (formerly SQL DW) charge per-DWU-hour. Pause pools during off-hours, weekends, and non-query periods. Storage charges continue but compute stops entirely.

tips.title: Scale down Synapse DWU level for lighter workloads
tips.description: Monitor query concurrency and performance to find the minimum DWU level that meets SLAs. Many workloads perform adequately at DW100c-DW500c instead of DW1000c or higher.

tips.title: Use Synapse Serverless SQL for ad-hoc queries
tips.description: Synapse Serverless SQL charges per-TB scanned instead of per-hour compute. Use it for ad-hoc exploration and lightweight queries, reserving dedicated pools for heavy production workloads.

tips.title: Optimize Data Factory integration runtime sizing
tips.description: Use auto-resolve integration runtime for light workloads instead of self-hosted. Right-size core count for data flows — start with 8 cores general-purpose and scale only if performance requires it.

tips.title: Set TTL on Data Factory data flow clusters
tips.description: Data flow clusters have a warm-up time but also charge while idle after job completion. Set TTL to 5-10 minutes to auto-terminate clusters quickly after pipeline runs finish.

tips.title: Use pipeline concurrency limits to control parallel costs
tips.description: Unbounded parallel pipeline runs can create expensive spikes in integration runtime costs. Set concurrency limits and queue excess runs to maintain predictable spending patterns.

tips.title: Use Spot instances for Databricks worker nodes
tips.description: Configure Databricks clusters to use Spot/Low-Priority VMs for worker nodes with on-demand driver. Provides 60-80% savings on cluster compute costs for fault-tolerant data engineering jobs.

tips.title: Configure aggressive auto-termination for Databricks clusters
tips.description: Set auto-termination to 10-20 minutes of inactivity. Use job clusters instead of interactive clusters for scheduled workloads — they terminate immediately after job completion.

tips.title: Use Photon runtime and right-size cluster nodes
tips.description: Photon-enabled clusters run queries 2-8x faster, allowing smaller clusters to handle the same workload. Profile jobs to find optimal node count and type instead of over-provisioning.

tips.title: Use Databricks SQL Serverless for BI queries
tips.description: Databricks SQL Serverless warehouses start instantly and scale to zero when idle. Better cost model for interactive BI queries than keeping dedicated SQL warehouses running 24/7.

tips.title: Use Azure Firewall Basic SKU for smaller deployments
tips.description: Azure Firewall Basic SKU costs significantly less than Standard/Premium for small businesses and branch offices. Provides core FQDN and network rules without premium features like IDPS.

tips.title: Deallocate Azure Firewall in non-production environments
tips.description: Azure Firewall charges per-hour even without traffic. Deallocate firewalls in dev/test environments after hours using Azure Automation. Reallocate on the same public IP when needed.

tips.title: Use shared Azure Firewall with hub-spoke topology
tips.description: Deploy a single Azure Firewall in the hub VNet rather than one per spoke. Route all spoke traffic through the centralized firewall to avoid multiple firewall deployment costs.

tips.title: Use DDoS Network Protection plan across subscriptions
tips.description: A single DDoS Protection plan can cover up to 100 public IPs across all VNets in a subscription. Share one plan across subscriptions instead of per-VNet protection to reduce costs.

tips.title: Evaluate if DDoS IP Protection suits your needs
tips.description: DDoS IP Protection covers individual public IPs at a lower per-IP cost than the Network Protection plan for fewer than ~10-15 IPs. Assess resource count to pick the cost-effective option.

tips.title: Consolidate WAF policies across Application Gateways
tips.description: Share WAF policies across multiple Application Gateway and Front Door instances rather than duplicating configurations. Reduces management overhead and can reduce per-policy charges.

tips.title: Use WAF on Front Door instead of per-region Application Gateway
tips.description: A single Azure Front Door with WAF can protect globally distributed apps cheaper than deploying WAF-enabled Application Gateways in each region.

tips.title: Optimize backup retention policies
tips.description: Review and reduce backup retention beyond compliance requirements. Keep daily backups for 7-14 days, weekly for 4-8 weeks, and monthly for 6-12 months instead of default aggressive retention.

tips.title: Move long-term backup data to Vault-Archive tier
tips.description: Azure Backup supports Vault-Archive tier for recovery points retained longer than 6 months. Saves up to 70% on backup storage for long-term compliance retention scenarios.

tips.title: Delete backups for decommissioned resources
tips.description: Backup vaults continue storing (and billing for) recovery points after source VMs or databases are deleted. Remove stale backup items and soft-deleted data after retention period.

tips.title: Remove unnecessary VMs from Site Recovery replication
tips.description: Azure Site Recovery charges per-protected-instance/month. Review which VMs truly need DR replication — dev/test and easily-recreatable VMs often don't justify the replication cost.

tips.title: Reduce Site Recovery snapshot retention
tips.description: Configure minimal recovery point retention (default is 24 hours, max 72). Reduce to the minimum acceptable RPO to lower the storage costs for replicated snapshots in the target region.

tips.title: Enforce cost-center and environment tags via Policy
tips.description: Deploy Azure Policy definitions that deny resource creation without mandatory tags (cost-center, environment, owner). Enables accurate chargeback and orphaned resource identification.

tips.title: Restrict expensive VM SKUs with Azure Policy
tips.description: Use Azure Policy to create an allowed VM sizes list. Block deployment of expensive GPU or memory-optimized SKUs unless explicitly justified. Prevents accidental expensive resource creation.

tips.title: Restrict allowed regions with Azure Policy
tips.description: Limit deployments to cost-effective regions using Azure Policy. Prevent accidental deployment to expensive regions and ensure resources stay co-located to avoid cross-region data transfer charges.

tips.title: Enforce tagging policies for cost allocation
tips.description: Use Azure Policy to enforce mandatory tags (cost-center, environment, owner) on all resources. Tags enable accurate chargeback, showback, and identification of unowned resources.
